title: Magnetoquantum Oscillations in the Specific Heat of a Topological Kondo Insulator
type1_summary: No replot-ready figure data files were provided in the inspected dataset contents.
type2_summary: No raw measurement files were provided in the inspected dataset contents.
classification_notes: The paper itself clearly contains both raw measurements and processed figure-ready analyses, but the inspected file list includes only the publication PDF. Since no actual data files are present, no Type 1 or Type 2 classification applies to the available file.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The provided paper analysis describes the publication itself, but no dataset repository contents beyond this PDF are available. The paper reports raw specific heat measurements and derived figure-ready analyses, but this file is the article, not a data file.
file_classifications.file_evidence: PDF article, 317.3 KB, from Zenodo. No tabular structure, headers, or machine-readable data content is provided in the inspection report.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is the publication/documentation file rather than a dataset file. It cannot be classified as Type 1 or Type 2 because it is not data; it serves as paper documentation.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports low-temperature specific heat measurements on the topological Kondo insulator SmB6, and a control measurement on LaB6, in high magnetic fields and varying field angle. The authors claim to observe magnetoquantum oscillations in the residual specific heat of SmB6 with frequencies consistent with prior torque data and argue that the oscillations are bulk thermodynamic in origin and incompatible with an aluminum-inclusion explanation; they also extract unusually large effective masses from the oscillation amplitude/node analysis.
measurement_types: specific heat C(H) vs magnetic field
Lomb-Scargle / Fourier analysis of oscillatory specific heat
control specific heat measurements on LaB6

figures.description: Temperature dependence of Cp/T for a 0.430 µg SmB6 sample at 0 T and 12 T for H || a axis, with a fit including Schottky, electronic, lattice, and anomalous low-T terms; inset shows field dependence of C/T at T = 0.99 K, including a Schottky peak near 1 T and suppression of gamma(H) above ~18-22 T.
figures.data_type: transport-like thermodynamic curve / specific heat curve

figures.description: Fourier power spectrum from oscillatory specific heat of LaB6 for a field sweep between 8 and 12 T at 0.358 K, with inset showing the inverse-field oscillatory specific heat used to generate the spectrum and an L-K fit.

Angular dependence of oscillation frequencies identified from Fourier spectra of SmB6 specific heat measured up to 32 T, compared with predicted angle-dependent frequencies from prior work; inset shows a representative Fourier power spectrum for H || [001] with a peak near 695 T.

Residual oscillatory specific heat of SmB6 versus inverse field at T = 0.518 K for phi = 45 degrees and a field sweep between 10 and 12 T, compared with an L-K model fit using the dominant frequency 325 T and effective mass meff = 6.6 me.

Model illustration showing how the oscillatory specific heat depends on effective mass for T = 0.58 K, TD = 1 K, and F = 341 T; traces are shown for several meff values to illustrate the node position.

Residual specific heat versus inverse field at T = 0.58 K and phi = 45 degrees, compared with an L-K fit using meff = 4.7 me and two frequencies (341 T and 1399 T) identified from the Fourier spectrum.

Comparison of the same SmB6 residual data with the oscillation amplitude expected for a 100% Al sample using Al effective mass meff = 0.13 me and TD = 1 K, demonstrating incompatibility with an aluminum-inclusion origin.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The raw measurements are low-temperature specific heat data C(T,H) collected on custom-built rotatable micro- and nano-calorimeters for SmB6 and LaB6. The paper states measurements were performed at T < 1 K in magnetic fields up to 32 T, and also as a function of temperature from 0.1 K to 100 K in fields up to 12 T. Bare calorimeter heat capacities were measured separately and subtracted, and thermometer magnetoresistance corrections were applied.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, downloadable dataset, or code archive is mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only as measured specific heat C(T,H) traces from custom rotatable micro- and nano-calorimeters, including field sweeps and temperature sweeps. The paper does not specify file formats, column structure, or whether raw traces are deposited anywhere.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include calorimeter-background-subtracted specific heat, thermometer magnetoresistance-corrected data, residual oscillatory specific heat after subtraction of a smooth non-oscillatory sigmoid background, Fourier/Lomb-Scargle power spectra, extracted oscillation frequencies, and fitted parameters such as effective mass, Dingle temperature, and gamma(H).
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly used: inverse-field residual oscillatory curves, Fourier power spectra, angle-dependent frequency points, and model comparison curves. However, the paper does not state that source data files for figures are provided separately.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: The analysis method is described in words: Lomb-Scargle frequency analysis, background subtraction using a sigmoid function, and Lifshitz-Kosevich fitting. No scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper distinguishes between raw calorimetry measurements and multiple layers of processing: subtraction of bare calorimeter heat capacity, thermometer correction, background subtraction, and Fourier analysis. The oscillation frequencies are extracted from processed residual data rather than directly from raw C(T,H). The text also includes theoretical/model-generated curves for L-K comparisons and for the aluminum-inclusion test.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The authors report direct measurements of C(T,H) on SmB6 and LaB6 using custom micro- and nano-calorimeters, with separate runs for bare calorimeters and thermometer corrections.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Multiple figures rely on processed residual specific heat, Fourier power spectra, and fitted/model curves; these are figure-ready derived datasets.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: Key phrases: 'Our measurements of C(T, H) were carried out using custom-built rotatable micro- and nano-calorimeters'; 'The heat capacities of the bare calorimeters were measured in separate runs and subtracted from the data'; 'corrections were also made for the magnetoresistance of the thermometers'; 'we fit the data to and then subtracted a non-oscillatory sigmoid function ... before carrying out the frequency analysis'; 'Applying the Lomb-Scargle frequency analysis described above'.

inspection_reports.note: PDF file, cannot inspect data content
